mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: neurological symptoms (shuffling gait, vertigo, difficulty standing), visceral involvement (hepatosplenomegaly, ascites, chylous effusions)
renal involvement (renal insufficiency, proteinuria), cardiac manifestations (cardiomyopathy, valvular heart disease), neuropathic symptoms (peripheral pain, autonomic dysfunction)

amenorrhea, antinuclear antibodies positive, pleurisy or pleural effusion, pericarditis with hemopericardium and chylopericardium, goiter, generalized muscle weakness, joint pain, Raynaud's phenomenon
recurrent UTIs due to platelet aggregation, easy bruising, elevated hepatic enzymes, splenomegaly, cough, palpitations, migraines, abdominal discomfort
respiratory issues (recurrent lung infections, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease), liver damage (elevated liver function tests), emphysema, clubbing of fingers, skin changes
goiter, xanthelasmas, tendon xanthomas, hyperlipidemia, peripheral polyneuropathy, myalgias, proximal muscle weakness
thyroid disorders (goiter, thyroiditis), cardiovascular problems (arrhythmias, cardiomegaly, cor pulmonale), pulmonary complications (pleural effusion, nonproductive cough)
microscopic hematuria, mild proteinuria, normal kidney size, systemic signs such as fever, malaise, arthralgia, rash
recurring fevers, serositis (abdominal pain, chest pain), erysipeloid-like cutaneous lesions, amyloidosis, inflammatory eye conditions
Marfan syndrome features (long thorax, tall stature, scoliosis), medullary carcinoma of the thyroid, phaeochromocytoma, paraganglioma
